name: Groq
description: Low-latency inference gateway (LPU) serving open-weight models via an OpenAI-compatible API

provider.comment: GroqCloud is operated by Groq, Inc., which designs the LPU inference hardware and hosts open-weight models on it.

category.comment: A low-latency inference gateway/host serving many vendors' open-weight models (Meta, OpenAI GPT-OSS, Moonshot, Qwen, MiniMax, Canopy) behind one OpenAI-compatible API with unified billing. Hosts its own inference of these models rather than routing to third-party APIs; not a first-party model trainer and not a hyperscaler cloud platform.

api-launched.comment: GroqCloud's self-serve developer API/console became publicly available in early 2024, but the exact GA month is not confirmed from a primary source fetched this pass (checked 2026-07).

status.comment: Core developer API (chat completions) is generally available with published pricing; some newer surfaces such as the Responses API are still in beta, and many non-core models are labelled preview (checked 2026-07).

api-docs.comment: Official GroqCloud developer documentation and API reference.

auth-methods.comment: Requests authenticated with a bearer API key (Authorization header) against the OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

self-serve-signup.comment: Fully self-serve — create a GroqCloud account and obtain a free API key without sales contact.

free-credits-trial.comment: A free tier is available ("Get started for free") with a free API key and rate-limited free usage for evaluation before moving to pay-as-you-go (checked 2026-07).

regional-endpoints.comment: Single global API endpoint (api.groq.com); no per-region self-serve endpoints exposed. Inference is primarily served from Groq's own US-based data centers (checked 2026-07).

data-residency-options.comment: No self-serve contractual data-residency / region-pinning guarantee documented; region-specific or on-premises deployment is only available via enterprise arrangements (checked 2026-07).

model-breadth.comment: Model families from many vendors reachable through one API: Meta (Llama), OpenAI (GPT-OSS), Moonshot (Kimi), Alibaba (Qwen), MiniMax, Canopy Labs (Orpheus).

open-weight-models.comment: Catalog is entirely open-weight models — Llama, GPT-OSS, Qwen, Kimi, MiniMax, Whisper, etc.; no proprietary/closed frontier models.

model-version-pinning.comment: Models are invoked by explicit versioned IDs (e.g. openai/gpt-oss-120b, moonshotai/kimi-k2-instruct-0905), so a specific snapshot can be pinned. Note: Groq deprecates/retires models aggressively (e.g. multiple 2026 deprecations), so pinned IDs can be sunset with notice.

custom-fine-tuned-deployment.comment: LoRA inference is supported, and enterprise arrangements allow deploying fine-tuned/custom models; not a fully self-serve custom-deploy flow for all users (checked 2026-07).

model-catalog-size.comment: Roughly 12–15 distinct callable models across production and preview tiers plus the Compound agentic systems (checked 2026-07); the catalog is curated and comparatively small versus large aggregators.

chat-messages-endpoint.comment: Modern OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint for multi-turn messages.

streaming.comment: Token streaming (SSE) supported via the OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint.

tool-function-calling.comment: Documented tool/function calling using the OpenAI-style tools parameter and tool_calls response; support is model-dependent.

parallel-tool-calls.comment: OpenAI-compatible tool calling supports multiple tool calls in a single turn (model-dependent); parallel tool calls follow the OpenAI Chat Completions semantics (checked 2026-07).

structured-output.comment: Strongest guarantee is server-enforced JSON Schema via response_format json_schema with strict:true (token-level constrained decoding that cannot produce invalid/off-schema output); the older json_object (valid-JSON) mode is also available. Availability is model-dependent, and the schema subset requires all properties to be marked required (checked 2026-07).

system-prompt.comment: Distinct system role supported via the OpenAI-compatible chat messages format.

image-input-vision.comment: Multimodal vision input supported (e.g. qwen/qwen3.6-27b), for VQA, captioning and OCR; up to 5 images and 20 MB per request. Model-dependent — the enabling vision model changes as the catalog turns over (checked 2026-07).

audio-input.comment: Speech-to-text/transcription and translation via Whisper Large V3 and V3 Turbo on the same API.

document-pdf-input.comment: No first-class native document/PDF input content type; images are accepted for vision/OCR but PDFs must be converted/extracted client-side (checked 2026-07).

image-generation.comment: No image-generation endpoint or model offered; Groq focuses on text, vision-input, and audio (checked 2026-07).

text-to-speech-audio-output.comment: Text-to-speech / audio synthesis offered on the same API (e.g. Orpheus V1 English and Orpheus Arabic Saudi TTS models).

embeddings-endpoint.comment: No text-embeddings endpoint or embedding model in the catalog as of the check (checked 2026-07).

prompt-caching.comment: Automatic prompt caching — cached input tokens are billed at a 50% discount on cache hits with no extra fee and no toggle required. Note: the beta Responses API does not yet support the prompt_cache_key parameter (checked 2026-07).

batch-api.comment: Asynchronous Batch API at 50% lower cost with a 24h–7d completion window, JSONL input files up to 100 MB, and separate rate limits; supports chat (text and vision), transcription and translation inputs.

max-context-window.comment: Largest served window is 196,608 tokens on the MiniMax M2.7 preview model; production models (Llama, GPT-OSS) top out at 131,072 tokens. Catalog turns over quickly, so the enabling model may change (checked 2026-07).

extended-reasoning.comment: Reasoning models are served with API-level reasoning controls (e.g. reasoning_format / reasoning-effort on GPT-OSS models, thinking vs non-thinking modes on Qwen); reasoning output is exposed at the API level.

built-in-web-search.comment: First-party built-in web search / browser-search tool available (via the Compound agentic systems and as a built-in browser search tool for GPT-OSS models in the Responses API); billed separately (checked 2026-07).

built-in-code-execution.comment: First-party code-execution / code-interpreter tool available (via Compound systems and the Responses API code interpreter for GPT-OSS models); billed separately (checked 2026-07).

mcp-support.comment: The Responses API supports remote MCP (Model Context Protocol) connectors to reach external systems/tools; Responses API is currently in beta (checked 2026-07).

files-retrieval-api.comment: A Files API exists for uploading batch JSONL inputs, but there is no managed retrieval/RAG service the model queries at inference time (checked 2026-07).

agents-assistants-api.comment: No stateful managed assistants/threads API — the Responses API explicitly does not yet support stateful conversations, so the caller must resend history each turn. Compound agentic systems bundle tools but are not a managed conversation-state service (checked 2026-07).

openai-compatible-api.comment: Exposes an OpenAI-Chat-Completions-compatible endpoint (base https://api.groq.com/openai/v1) usable with the OpenAI SDK via a base-URL swap; a Responses API compatible with OpenAI's is also offered in beta.

official-sdks.comment: First-party Python (groq) and TypeScript/JavaScript (groq-sdk) SDKs; other languages (C#, Dart, PHP, Ruby) are community-contributed and not officially verified. OpenAI SDKs also work against the compatible endpoint.

native-api-style.comment: Primary request/response shape follows the OpenAI Chat Completions (and OpenAI Responses) style.

webhooks-callbacks.comment: Batch job completion can be delivered via webhook notification in addition to polling batch status (checked 2026-07).

published-sla.comment: No public contractual uptime SLA figure found from a primary source this pass; SLA terms are enterprise-negotiated. Third-party monitors report ~99.9% observed uptime, but that is not a contractual SLA (checked 2026-07).

rate-limit-model.comment: Fixed per-model rate limits by account tier (free vs on-demand), with additional service tiers (on-demand, flex, auto) affecting throughput/queueing; enterprise/committed-spend contracts are negotiated separately (checked 2026-07).

provider-fallback-routing.comment: Groq hosts and serves its own model deployments on its LPU hardware; there is no automatic cross-provider failover/policy routing like a pure aggregator (checked 2026-07).

status-page.comment: Public status page exists at status.groq.com (mirrored at groqstatus.com), tracking website and API/endpoint availability (checked 2026-07).

no-training-default.comment: Groq is not permitted to use Inputs or Outputs to train or fine-tune models unless the customer explicitly grants permission; not used for training by default.

zero-data-retention.comment: Inference requests are not retained by default; only temporary troubleshooting/abuse logs (up to 30 days, opt-out available) may be kept. All customers can additionally enable a Zero Data Retention (ZDR) setting in Data Controls (checked 2026-07).

compliance-certifications.comment: SOC 2 Type II reports maintained (via Trust Center / Vanta monitoring); GroqCloud stated SOC 2, GDPR and HIPAA compliant, with a DPA covering GDPR/CCPA/PDPL. ISO 27001, FedRAMP and PCI not confirmed from the fetched sources this pass (checked 2026-07).

enterprise-sso-access-control.comment: Could not confirm SSO/SAML and org-level console access controls from a fetched primary source this pass; not documented in the pages reviewed (checked 2026-07).

pricing-model.comment: Per-token pay-as-you-go usage pricing (input and output priced per million tokens per model) with no minimum commitment, plus a free tier to start; enterprise/on-prem arrangements available separately.

unified-billing.comment: One GroqCloud account/bill covers all reachable models and built-in tools.

cache-batch-discount.comment: 50% discount on cached input tokens (prompt caching) and 50% lower cost on the Batch API. Note the two discounts do not stack — batch tokens are billed at the flat 50% batch rate regardless of cache status (checked 2026-07).

cheapest-entry.comment: Lowest-friction start is the self-serve free tier (free API key with rate-limited usage) before moving to pay-as-you-go.
